subject: [PATCH v2 2/3] mm: swap: bypass synchronous readahead unless the target needs I/O

2026-07-22.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's swap readahead behavior to bypass synchronous readahead unless the target needs I/O. This change is made to improve performance in certain scenarios, such as when the faulting target is also served synchronously from a device. The patch introduces a trade-off between initial fault latency and throughput gain, with results showing improved build times and reduced swap readahead for zswap. The changes are made to the mm/swap_state.c file, specifically in the swap_cluster_readahead and swap_vma_readahead functions.
